user_query:
Dear friend, could you build me a small event‑triggered automation for my weekly sermons that runs locally and produces concrete files? Please use the inputs under input/ and write all outputs under output/ as specified. Treat a sermon as "new" if it is not listed in output/state/processed.json or if its content hash has changed.

Inputs provided:
- Sermon drafts (Markdown with YAML front matter): input/sermons/*.md
- A rough announcement template: input/templates/announcement.txt (contains {{title}}, {{passage}}, {{series}}, {{date}}, {{summary}})
- Lint/render logs (simulated command output): input/logs/<YYYY-MM-DD>-render.log (date matches the sermon front matter)

For each new sermon file input/sermons/<slug>.md (slug = filename without extension):
1) Fill the template with values parsed from the sermon’s front matter, then rewrite that filled text into a warm, clear, story‑minded bulletin announcement of 280–400 characters, pastoral in tone and concise. Include the sermon title once (in quotes) and the scripture passage once. Do not include internal notes or the word "WARNING"/"ERROR" in the announcement. Save as output/announcements/<slug>.txt.
2) Draft a congregation email and save it as output/emails/<slug>.txt with these exact header lines at the top, followed by a blank line and the body:
   - To: congregation@example.org
   - Subject: either "[HOLD] <date> — <title>" if any ERROR is present in the corresponding log (input/logs/<date>-render.log), or "<date> — <title>" otherwise.
   Email body must include:
   - The announcement text from step 1.
   - A single line labeled "Story hook:" followed by a hook of at most 20 words distilled from the first sentence of the summary.
   - If the log has any WARNING or ERROR lines, append a clearly labeled "Internal Note (for pastor only):" section that lists the total counts (e.g., "2 warnings, 1 error") and up to the first three WARNING/ERROR lines verbatim from the log.
3) Analyze the corresponding log file input/logs/<date>-render.log to compute counts. Write output/status/<slug>.status containing exactly "ON_HOLD" if there is at least one ERROR, else "READY". Also append a single run line to output/logs/run.log in the format: "<slug> WARNINGS=<n> ERRORS=<m> STATUS=<READY|ON_HOLD>".
4) Maintain state in output/state/processed.json as a JSON object with a list of processed items. Each item must include slug, source_path, sha256 of the sermon file, and processed_at (ISO timestamp). On subsequent runs, only process files not present or whose sha256 has changed; update state accordingly.

Additional notes:
- Do not send email; only write files.
- Assume the matching log for a sermon is input/logs/<date>-render.log, where <date> comes from the sermon’s front matter date field.
- Please run the automation once on the provided inputs so that the outputs are created:
  • output/announcements/2026-04-19-sermon.txt
  • output/announcements/2026-04-26-sermon.txt
  • output/emails/2026-04-19-sermon.txt
  • output/emails/2026-04-26-sermon.txt
  • output/status/2026-04-19-sermon.status
  • output/status/2026-04-26-sermon.status
  • output/state/processed.json (with both items recorded)
  • output/logs/run.log (with two lines)

This will help me share each message with warmth and clarity, and it should hold outgoing notes if the draft needs attention per the logs. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
